description: tilize + reduce_row(mean) + sub(COL broadcast) + untilize. Full reader/writer data pipeline.

approach: TensorAccessor for RM sticks, prepare_reduce_scaler(1/W) for cb2, prepare_reduce_scaler(eps) for cb3, batch 32 sticks per block push Wt pages

tilize(cb0->cb1), reduce_row SUM WaitUpfrontNoPop(cb1,cb2->cb4), sub COL NoWaitNoPop/WaitAndPop(cb1,cb4->cb16), manual pop cb1, untilize UnpackAndPackReconfig(cb16->cb17). Stage1 bypass: output to cb16 directly instead of cb5.
TensorAccessor for output RM sticks, wait Wt pages cb17, write 32 sticks per block via noc_async_write, pop Wt pages

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/Wt(wait_upfront+manual_pop) c2:1/persist c4:1/1 c16:Wt/Wt c17:Wt/Wt

design_deviations: Stage1 bypass: sub output to cb16 directly instead of cb5 since phases 4-7 not yet implemented

description: Add square, reduce_row(var), add_eps+rsqrt post_op, mul_inv_std. Full layer norm without affine.

approach: Added phases 4-7: square(WaitUpfrontNoPop cb5->cb6), reduce_row(WaitAndPopPerTile cb6->cb7), add_eps+rsqrt_post_op(cb7+cb3->cb24), mul_inv_std(SCALAR NoWaitNoPop cb5*cb24->cb16). Phase 3 output now to cb_centered(cb5) instead of cb16. Manual pop cb5 after phase7. cb_eps waited at start, popped at end.

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/Wt(upfront+manual_pop) c2:1/persist c3:1/persist(wait_start,pop_end) c4:1/1 c5:Wt/Wt(upfront+manual_pop) c6:Wt/Wt c7:1/1 c16:Wt/Wt c17:Wt/Wt c24:1/1

details: max diff 1.6875 on 1x1x32x32. Output values are scaled wrong -- e.g. expected -0.8984 got -0.9180, expected 1.9297 got 2.9531. Values are close in sign but magnitudes differ systematically, suggesting inv_std computation is wrong.

description: epsilon tile prepared with prepare_reduce_scaler fills row0 of all faces. SCALAR broadcast binary_add expects scalar at [0,0] only. Extra values in row0 corrupt the add result, leading to wrong variance+eps, wrong rsqrt, wrong output.
evidence: systematic magnitude errors in output, prepare_reduce_scaler fills 16 cols in row0 per face but SCALAR bcast only needs [0,0]

description: Design error: Phase 7 mul_inv_std uses SCALAR broadcast but inv_std is per-row (col0 from REDUCE_ROW). SCALAR bcast uses only [0][0]=rsqrt(var_row0+eps) for ALL rows. Need COL broadcast to apply each rows rsqrt(var+eps) correctly. Similarly Phase 6 add_eps was fine since SCALAR bcast of eps is correct but result needs to stay col0-valid for COL bcast in Phase 7.
evidence: 1x1x32x32 shows systematic errors with different magnitudes per row - first row values close (using correct inv_std) but later rows diverge (using wrong inv_std from row 0)

change: Phase 7 mul_inv_std changed from BroadcastDim::SCALAR to BroadcastDim::COL. inv_std is REDUCE_ROW output with per-row values in col0, needs COL broadcast to apply correct per-row normalization.

design_deviations: Phase 7 mul_inv_std changed from SCALAR to COL broadcast - design error, REDUCE_ROW output is per-row in col0 requiring COL broadcast

description: Add gamma/beta reads in reader, mul(ROW) and add(ROW) phases 8-9 in compute. Program-lifetime cb_gamma/cb_beta.

change: Added ttnn.tilize() for gamma/beta before passing to program descriptor. Tiled tensors passed to create_program_descriptor and io_tensors.
reason: Reader needs tile-sized pages for gamma/beta CBs. RM tensors have stick-sized pages that dont match CB tile page size. Tilizing on host side is simplest approach.

change: Added TensorAccessorArgs for gamma/beta to reader compile-time args with start index markers. Layout: [stick_size, input_accessor..., gamma_start_idx, gamma_accessor..., beta_start_idx, beta_accessor...]
reason: Reader needs TensorAccessor to read gamma/beta tiles from DRAM interleaved pages

change: Restructured reader CT args: [0]=stick_size, [1]=gamma_accessor_start(0 if none), [2]=beta_accessor_start(0 if none), [3+]=input_accessor, then gamma/beta accessor args appended. Input accessor now starts at index 3 instead of 1.
reason: Clean fixed-position layout so kernel can find gamma/beta accessor start indices at known compile-time positions

approach: Updated CT arg layout: [0]=stick_size [1]=gamma_acc_start [2]=beta_acc_start [3+]=input_acc. Input accessor now at index 3. Added gamma/beta tile reads using TensorAccessor with if constexpr on accessor start indices. Reads Wt tiles each into cb_gamma/cb_beta at startup.

Added Phase 8 mul_gamma(ROW bcast, WaitAndPopPerTile A, NoWaitNoPop B, in-place cb16) and Phase 9 add_beta(ROW bcast, same pattern). Program-lifetime cb_gamma/cb_beta waited at start with if constexpr, popped at end. Both use NoWaitNoPop on B since tiles persist across blocks.

cb_summary: c0-c7,c16,c17,c24: same as full_normalize. c25(gamma):Wt/persist(wait_start,NoWaitNoPop per block,pop_end). c26(beta):Wt/persist(same). c16 in-place: phase7 pushes Wt -> phase8 pops/pushes Wt -> phase9 pops/pushes Wt -> untilize waits Wt.

details: TT_FATAL: Input tensor physical volume (32) must be divisible by TILE_HW (1024). ttnn.tilize() fails on gamma (1,1,1,32) because 32 elements < 1024 tile elements. Need to pad gamma/beta to (1,1,32,W) before tilizing.

description: ttnn.tilize requires physical volume divisible by 1024. Gamma (1,1,1,W) has W elements which is only 32 for minimal shape. Need to pad H dim to 32 before tilize so shape becomes (1,1,32,W) = W*32 >= 1024 elements.
evidence: TT_FATAL physical volume 32 not divisible by TILE_HW 1024

change: Added ttnn.pad to expand gamma/beta from (1,1,1,W) to (1,1,32,W) before ttnn.tilize. Pads H dim by 31 rows of zeros so volume >= TILE_HW(1024).

triage: Hang on 1x1x64x128 (Wt=4). First shape 1x1x32x32 (Wt=1) passed. All-zero output suggests kernel never completed.
suspected_cause: In-place CB deadlock: Phase 8/9 use Bulk output on cb_out_pre_untilize which tries to reserve Wt pages upfront but CB is full with Wt input tiles from phase 7. Wt=1 works because pop frees space before reserve, but Wt>1 deadlocks.

description: In-place CB deadlock for Wt>1: Phase 8/9 use BinaryOutputPolicy::Bulk which reserves Wt output pages upfront. But cb_out_pre_untilize already has Wt input tiles from phase 7, and CB only has Wt total pages. Bulk reserve deadlocks waiting for space. Fix: use PerTile output policy so each tile is reserved after the previous input tile is popped.
evidence: Wt=1 passes (single tile, pop frees space before reserve), Wt=4 hangs (4 tiles fill CB, bulk reserve for 4 more deadlocks)

change: Phase 8 and 9 changed from BinaryOutputPolicy::Bulk to BinaryOutputPolicy::PerTile for in-place CB safety. PerTile reserves 1 output page after popping each input tile, avoiding deadlock when Wt>1.

description: tilize + reduce_row mean + broadcast COL subtract + untilize

approach: TensorAccessor for RM sticks, prepare_reduce_scaler, prepare_eps, gamma/beta tile reads
tilize + reduce_row(mean) + sub(COL bcast) + untilize passthrough
TensorAccessor for RM stick writes, 32 sticks per block

description: add variance computation (square + reduce_row) + add_eps + rsqrt + mul_inv_std COL bcast

approach: added phases 4-7: square(centered), reduce_row(var), add_eps+rsqrt(inv_std), mul_inv_std(COL bcast)

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/Wt c2:1/persist c3:1/persist c4:1/1 c5:Wt/Wt c6:Wt/Wt c7:1/1 c16:Wt/Wt c17:Wt/Wt c24:1/1

details: max diff 1.6875 - Phase 7 used SCALAR broadcast but REDUCE_ROW output has per-row values in col0, only row 0 inv_std applied to all rows

description: Phase 7 mul_inv_std uses SCALAR broadcast but REDUCE_ROW output stores per-row values in column 0. Need COL broadcast to correctly distribute each rows inv_std across all columns.
evidence: numerical error pattern - only row 0 normalization correct

change: changed Phase 7 mul broadcast from SCALAR to COL - design doc error corrected

design_deviations: Phase 7: changed SCALAR broadcast to COL broadcast - design doc error, REDUCE_ROW output has per-row values in col0

description: add optional gamma mul (ROW bcast) and beta add (ROW bcast) phases 8-9

approach: added phases 8-9: mul_gamma(ROW bcast, PerTile output in-place on cb_out_pre_untilize) and add_beta(ROW bcast, PerTile output in-place)
added gamma/beta tile reads using TensorAccessor with compile-time ct_start guard

change: added ttnn.pad + ttnn.tilize for gamma/beta - pad (1,1,1,W) to (1,1,32,W) before tilize since tilize requires volume >= TILE_HW (1024)
reason: ttnn.tilize fails on (1,1,1,32) with physical volume 32 not divisible by TILE_HW 1024

change: added gamma/beta TensorAccessorArgs to reader compile-time args with dynamic ct_start indices
reason: reader kernel needs TensorAccessor compile-time args for gamma/beta TILE_LAYOUT tensors

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/Wt c2:1/persist c3:1/persist c4:1/1 c5:Wt/Wt c6:Wt/Wt c7:1/1 c16:Wt/Wt(in-place gamma+beta) c17:Wt/Wt c24:1/1 c25:Wt/persist c26:Wt/persist

details: TT_FATAL physical volume (32) not divisible by TILE_HW (1024) when tilizing gamma (1,1,1,32)

description: ttnn.tilize requires tensor volume >= TILE_HW (1024). Gamma (1,1,1,W) has volume W which can be < 1024. Need to pad H dimension to 32 before tilize.
evidence: TT_FATAL message explicitly says physical volume 32 not divisible by 1024

change: added ttnn.pad((0,0),(0,0),(0,31),(0,0)) before ttnn.tilize for gamma and beta

triage: compute hung on in-place CB operation for shape 1x1x64x128 (Wt=4)
suspected_cause: Bulk output policy deadlocks when CB sized for Wt pages is already full with Wt input tiles

description: In-place CB reuse with Bulk output policy deadlocks for Wt>1. Bulk reserves all Wt output pages upfront but CB already has Wt input pages. PerTile output reserves 1 page after popping each input tile, avoiding deadlock.
evidence: works for Wt=1 (1x1x32x32) but hangs for Wt=4 (1x1x64x128)

change: changed Phases 8 and 9 from BinaryOutputPolicy::Bulk to BinaryOutputPolicy::PerTile for in-place CB safety

upstream_fixes: added ttnn.pad+tilize for gamma/beta in entry point
added gamma/beta TensorAccessorArgs to reader CT args in program descriptor
design_deviations: Phases 8-9: changed BinaryOutputPolicy from Bulk to PerTile for in-place CB reuse safety
